daily_brief: The fragile US-Iran ceasefire dominates global attention today as President Trump declared the month-old truce is on 'massive life support' after rejecting Tehran's 14-point counter-proposal as 'totally unacceptable' [BBC]. Iran's parliamentary speaker responded that armed forces are 'ready to respond' to any aggression [BBC], while France and the UK convene over 40 defence ministers to plan military operations to restore shipping through the blocked Strait of Hormuz [RFI]. The diplomatic uncertainty has rattled Asian markets, with Japanese 10-year bond yields hitting their highest level since 1997 on inflation fears [Nikkei Asia]. Meanwhile, all eyes turn to Beijing where President Trump arrives Wednesday for a consequential three-day summit with Xi Jinping, accompanied by major US CEOs including Elon Musk and Tim Cook [The Hindu, Al Jazeera]. In Europe, UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer faces an existential political crisis as cabinet ministers reportedly urge him to consider his departure following devastating local election losses [Financial Times, Al Jazeera]. The EU has finally agreed sanctions on Israeli settlers after Hungary's new government dropped its veto [RFI], while the hantavirus outbreak on cruise ship MV Hondius continues to spread internationally with new cases confirmed in France and the US [BBC, Globe and Mail].

top_stories.headline: Trump Declares US-Iran Ceasefire on 'Massive Life Support' as Tehran Rejects Counter-Proposal
bullets: President Trump told reporters the month-old ceasefire with Iran is 'unbelievably weak' and rejected Iran's 14-point counter-proposal as 'totally unacceptable' and a 'piece of garbage'
Iran's parliamentary speaker Mohammad Ghalibaf responded on X that Iran's armed forces are 'ready to respond and to teach a lesson for any aggression'

Tehran's proposal reportedly includes demands for an end to Israeli attacks on Hezbollah in Lebanon, lifting of the US naval blockade, guarantees against future attacks, and compensation for war damage
France and UK are co-hosting a 40-nation virtual meeting of defence ministers to plan multinational military operations to restore shipping through the blocked Strait of Hormuz

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Lead coverage focused on Trump's comments and Iranian response, detailed the 14-point proposal

sources.name: RFI English
sources.framing_note: Emphasized the France-UK diplomatic initiative and multinational coalition-building

sources.name: France 24
sources.framing_note: Provided live coverage focusing on military planning aspects

sources.name: The Hindu
sources.framing_note: Included detail about UAE secretly conducting strikes on Iran per Wall Street Journal report

top_stories.headline: Trump to Meet Xi in Beijing This Week in Most Consequential US-China Summit in Years
bullets: President Trump arrives in Beijing Wednesday for a three-day summit with President Xi Jinping, the first US presidential visit to China since 2017
Major US CEOs including Elon Musk (Tesla), Tim Cook (Apple), and leaders of Boeing, Goldman Sachs, and Citi will accompany Trump
Key agenda items include the US-Israel war on Iran, trade relations, Taiwan tensions, and competition in advanced technologies including AI and rare earth minerals
China is positioning itself as a potential peacemaker in the Iran conflict, having joined Pakistan in mediation efforts

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Emphasized the summit's potential to set US-China relations for years, noted China's diplomatic opportunity

sources.name: Al Jazeera
sources.framing_note: Provided historical context of all seven Trump-Xi meetings since 2017

sources.name: Nikkei Asia
sources.framing_note: Focused on economic angles including rare earth minerals and trade implications

sources.name: The Hindu
sources.framing_note: Highlighted the business delegation and bilateral trade tensions

top_stories.headline: UK Prime Minister Starmer Faces Cabinet Revolt as Senior Ministers Urge Him to Consider Departure
bullets: Interior Minister Shabana Mahmood and at least two other cabinet ministers have reportedly told Starmer to consider setting out a timeline for his departure
More than 80 Labour MPs have called for Starmer's resignation following devastating local election losses last week where Labour lost over 1,400 seats
Six ministerial aides resigned on Monday; UK government bonds and sterling dropped amid political uncertainty
Starmer remains defiant, stating Labour would 'never be forgiven' for the chaos of constantly changing leaders, and delivered a speech pledging to bring Britain 'back to the heart of Europe'

sources.name: Financial Times
sources.framing_note: Focused on market reaction and political manoeuvring around Chancellor Rachel Reeves

sources.name: Al Jazeera
sources.framing_note: Emphasized the scale of opposition within Labour Party

sources.name: RFI English
sources.framing_note: Highlighted Starmer's pro-European messaging as political strategy

top_stories.headline: Hantavirus Outbreak Spreads Internationally as Final Cruise Ship Passengers Evacuated
bullets: All remaining passengers have been evacuated from MV Hondius in Tenerife; three passengers have died from the outbreak
Seven confirmed cases and two suspected cases have been linked to the ship according to WHO; new positive cases confirmed in France and the US
A French woman's health is reported to be deteriorating with 22 contacts traced; a second American showed mild symptoms during repatriation
WHO Director-General stated the public 'should know there is nothing to fear, the risk is low, this is not another COVID'

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Comprehensive coverage of evacuation logistics and case tracking

sources.name: Globe and Mail
sources.framing_note: Included WHO reassurance about public health risk

top_stories.headline: EU Approves Sanctions on Israeli Settlers After Hungary Drops Long-Standing Veto
bullets: The European Union agreed to new sanctions on Israeli settlers in the occupied West Bank for violence against Palestinians
Sanctions target three individual settlers and four settler organisations with asset freezes and travel bans
Hungary's new Prime Minister Peter Magyar lifted the veto that predecessor Viktor Orbán had maintained for months
Sanctions will also target Hamas members, a condition required by some EU countries for their support

sources.name: RFI English
sources.framing_note: Quoted EU foreign policy chief Kallas saying 'it was high time we move from deadlock to delivery'

additional_stories.headline: France announces €23 billion in African investments at Kenya summit, signaling shift away from former colonial ties
UN reports drones now cause over 80% of civilian deaths in Sudan conflict, warns of 'deadlier phase'
Japanese 10-year bond yields hit highest level since 1997 as Iran war stokes inflation fears
US holds closely guarded talks with Denmark to open three new military bases in southern Greenland
Nearly 200 sanctioned Russian shadow fleet vessels entered UK waters since Starmer threatened interceptions
UN report confirms at least 269 killed in Pakistani airstrike on Afghan drug rehabilitation centre
BBC investigation unmasks 'Kardo Ranya,' key people smuggler behind majority of Channel crossings
Southern California mayor Eileen Wang resigns, agrees to plead guilty to acting as illegal agent for Chinese government
India declines Russian LNG under sanctions despite energy shortfall from Middle East tensions
Mitsubishi Heavy expects profit surge as Japan eases restrictions on defence equipment exports
